On Comets XI 133

II From these statements of the Sage we come to know that
Vasaketu, Asthiketu and Sastraketu are included among the sons
of Sukra (Venus). The first of these makes its appearance once
in every 3,000 years, Kapalaketu is one of the children of the Sun.
It appears once in every 2500 years,]
STT^^TT^TTHTT ^TPCT: I
Raudra is another comet, rising in the east, whose effects are
similar to those of Kapalaketu, It traverses in the Dahanavjthi
(IX-1,3), Its crest is in the form of a trident^ and its flame grey,
rough and red. It traverses a third of the sky.
